sample_questions.q: When should you prefer a while loop over for?
sample_questions.a: When the number of iterations isn't known in advance, e.g., reading until EOF or polling a condition.

sample_questions.q: What's the difference between break and continue?
sample_questions.a: break exits the enclosing loop entirely; continue skips to the next iteration of the same loop.
Ethos is the appeal to the speaker's credibility, character, or authority on the topic.

sample_questions.q: Why does audience matter when analyzing an argument?
sample_questions.a: Because the same claim can be persuasive or alienating depending on the audience's values, prior knowledge, and stakes.
The unstated assumption that connects the evidence to the claim — what the audience must accept for the argument to work.
A water-potential gradient — water moves from regions of higher water potential (lower solute concentration) to lower.

sample_questions.q: Is the Na+/K+ pump active or passive transport?
sample_questions.a: Active — it uses ATP to move ions against their concentration gradients (3 Na+ out, 2 K+ in).

sample_questions.q: Why is the bilayer described as a 'fluid mosaic'?
sample_questions.a: Because the lipids and embedded proteins move laterally within the membrane, giving it both fluid and patchwork character.
